summary_one_line: A structurally valid DTD whose external subset reaches xmlAddAttributeDecl with dtd->last==NULL, i.e. the first declaration linked into a fresh subset.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that drives the SAX parser through xmlSAX2AttributeDecl into xmlAddAttributeDecl on the external subset (ctxt->inSubset==2) at a point where the DTD's child list is still empty, so the dtd->last==NULL true branch fires (line 2164 -> 2165). This requires assembling a well-formed XML document with a DOCTYPE/external-subset declaration plus a syntactically valid first <!ATTLIST> declaration, including varied element/attribute structure (e.g. xlink attributes, alternate encodings, nested elements) that the cmplog fixed template never produces.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winner seeds carry diverse, structurally recombined XML: ISO-8859-5 encoding decls, nested '<a><b xlink="">' elements, varied DOCTYPE/SYSTEM dtds references, and processing-instruction variants. The W role hits xmlCheckDefaultedAttributes (44 vs 0), xmlSAX2StartElement (44 vs 0) and xmlSAX2AttributeInternal (28 vs 0), and reaches line 2165 (dtd->children=dtd->last=ret), confirming the first-declaration true branch executes on the external subset. Branch L2164 shows W T=27 F=9 vs L T=0 F=66 — the winner takes the true side 27 times while the loser never does.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: All five cmplog loser seeds are near-identical fixed boilerplate ('<?xml version="1.0"?>' + '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772...') sharing the same 06 00 00 00 prefix and only trivial byte-level mutation (DwordInteresting, ByteDec/ByteAdd, BytesSwap). cmplog's I2S + havoc cannot synthesize the structural variation needed to enter the external subset with an empty child list, so L2164 is taken false 66 times and never true; the loser instead routes through xmlSAX2ElementDecl/xmlAddElementDecl (0 vs 57 in W) and xmlValidateAttributeDecl (0 vs 9), a different declaration-handling path that leaves dtd->last already populated.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus XML into <GAP> token structures and the Grimoire structural mutators (GrimoireExtensionMutator, GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator) splice/recurse on those grammar-free token structures, generating the well-formed-yet-novel DTD/ATTLIST arrangements that reach xmlAddAttributeDecl on a fresh external subset. cmplog has the same I2S stage but lacks generalization + structural recombination, so it stays stuck on byte-level edits of one template.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true branch of dtd->last==NULL inside xmlAddAttributeDecl, fired when the first declaration is linked into the subset.
Only the winner executes the first-declaration assignment dtd->children=dtd->last=ret at line 2165.
At the blocker the winner takes the true side 27 times while the loser takes it zero times (false 66).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1  L2164  T=27 F=9  T=0 F=66  if (dtd->last == NULL) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is grimoire>cmplog whose single-technique delta is grimoire_structural; grimoire adds generalization + Grimoire mutators on top of cmplog's I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire winner seeds contain structurally recombined XML such as nested elements with xlink attributes.
Grimoire winner seeds carry alternate encoding declarations such as ISO-8859-5.
Loser cmplog seeds are near-identical fixed boilerplate sharing the same 06 00 00 00 prefix and 127772.xml template.
Loser cmplog seeds only receive trivial byte-level havoc mutations, not structural recombination.
The winner exercises element-start and defaulted-attribute SAX paths that the loser never enters.
The loser instead routes through xmlAddElementDecl, a different declaration path not taken by the winner.
Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators perform grammar-free structural recombination of token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is grimoire structural (GeneralizationStage + Grimoire mutators), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory I2S dash-row lineage check does not apply. The decisive delta grimoire_structural is established directly from the MECHANISM CONTEXT (both grimoire and cmplog already run I2S, so I2S cannot be the differentiator) and from the seed evidence showing structural recombination in winner seeds versus fixed-template byte havoc in loser seeds. No lineage query was required for this mechanism.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which grimoire and cmplog reach xmlAddAttributeDecl's dtd->last==NULL true branch at statistically indistinguishable rates, showing structural recombination provides no advantage over cmplog's I2S+havoc for assembling the first valid <!ATTLIST> declaration in a fresh external subset.

weakest_evidence_point: The seed-provenance note warns the storing fuzzer may differ from the decisive winner/loser, so attributing the winner seeds' structural diversity specifically to grimoire's mutators rests partly on the mutation_op chains rather than a controlled per-fuzzer comparison.
